title: DTC Explanation: P0201, P0202, P0203, P21CF, P21D0, P21D1

blocks.text: DTC Explanation: P0201, P0202, P0203, P21CF, P21D0, P21D1

DTC P0201: No.1 Injector circuit failure DTC P0202: No.2 Injector circuit failure DTC P0203: No.3 Injector circuit failure DTC P21CF: No.1 Injector circuit failure (secondary) DTC P21DF: No.2 Injector circuit failure (secondary) DTC P21D1: No.3 Injector circuit failure (secondary)

Fuel injectors supply fuel to the engine under the control of the PGM-FI ECU; the fuel injectors are driven by ON/OFF drive commands output from the PGM-FI ECU for optimal fuel injection. The fuel injector operation status is monitored by the return circuit inside the PGM-FI ECU. If the return signal from the fuel injector does not change in response to a drive command for a set number of times, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
If the return signal from the fuel injectors (counted once per engine cycle) remains unchanged for more than 27 cycles.

NO.1 Fuel injector (primary) failure

PGM-FI ECU internal circuit failure

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
